node.title: Uber | Onsite | Number of squares formed by perpendicular lines

topic.title: Uber | Onsite | Number of squares formed by perpendicular lines

post.content:
O(n^2) to find list of intersecting points. O(n^2) to find the squares from points in a hashmap or set. It can actually be done in O(1.5n)
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/3831144/finding-the-squares-in-a-plane-given-n-points

Overall O(n^2)

Boils down to 2 steps - One find all intersections in O(n^2) time. For every pair of Line- Perpendicular Line we can find the intersection points, Store this in a set . For every point in the set, compare with every other point to see if they form a square \n\nO(n^2) + O(n^2) = O(n^2)
Using a hashmap you can easily reduce from O(n4) to O(n3).
Did you interview in Sydney by any chance?

O(N^3) is not possible in the worst case I think. consider all the horizontal line ``` y = k``` with vertical line ```x = k```, k is an integer. There are literally ```O(n^4)``` squares you need to check.

Not all pairs need to be checked though:

1. O(N^2) to store pairs of horizonal/vertical lines in two maps, with their distance as key.
2. use (1) to only consider pairs of lines with a fixed distance.
